name: Punjabi
register: Reverent contemporary Punjabi in the wording family of the Pavittar Bibal / Punjabi Bible (Indian Bible Society) tradition — the version Punjabi Christian congregations read aloud and pray from — using the affectionate-respectful honorific address (ਜੀ) that a Punjabi child uses for their own parent. Warm and devotional, not colloquial slang, not stiff liturgical Sanskritized diction.
quote_convention: Double quotation marks “ ” for speech; single ‘ ’ for a quote nested inside a quote (following modern Punjabi Bible typesetting).
poetry_convention: Poetry set as line-broken verse with each poetic line on its own line and second-half parallel (colon) lines indented one step; Hebrew/Greek parallelism preserved rather than flattened into prose.

glossary.note: Filial divine name. ਸੁਰਗੀ 'heavenly' from ਸੁਰਗ, the Christian Punjabi word for heaven; ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ = 'Father' + the honorific ਜੀ a Punjabi child uses for their own parent — reverent AND affectionate, not the generic ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ.
Primary filial divine name — the ਪਿਤਾ of the Punjabi Lord's Prayer (‘ਹੇ ਸਾਡੇ ਪਿਤਾ’), warmed with ਜੀ. NEVER revert to ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ (God) or ਯਹੋਵਾਹ (LORD).
Definite filial name. Bare ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ carries the definite sense in Punjabi; ਬਾਪ would be blunt, ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ would erase the filial vision.
Filial name without the possessive; same ਸੁਰਗੀ ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ base as 'our Heavenly Father' minus ਸਾਡੇ.
Aramaic ਅੱਬਾ retained by transliteration, then glossed with the standard filial ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ — mirrors the English 'Abba, Father' double-naming and keeps machine-checkable stem ਪਿਤਾ.
NON-FILIAL contrast term. Traditional Punjabi Bible word for God-as-God (ਖ਼ੁਦਾ / ਰੱਬ are the everyday alternatives). Use only where English keeps 'God'; never as a substitute for ਸਾਡੇ ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ.
NON-FILIAL contrast term — the covenant name as spelled in the Punjabi Bible. Use only where English keeps 'the LORD'. Generic 'Lord' for Jesus is the distinct ਪ੍ਰਭੂ (Prabhu).
Standard Punjabi Bible term; ਪਵਿੱਤਰ 'holy' + ਆਤਮਾ 'spirit'.
Relational rendering (of-the-Father), NOT identity ('the Spirit who is God'); preserves the filial stem ਪਿਤਾ rather than collapsing to ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ ਦਾ ਆਤਮਾ.

glossary.gloss: the Son of Man (of the human being)
glossary.note: Standard Punjabi Bible title; ਪੁੱਤਰ is the warm/native word for son (vs. Sanskritic ਸੁਤ).
The Son (Jesus). ਪੁੱਤਰ is the affectionate Punjabi word a parent uses for their child — the filial warmth mirrors ਪਿਤਾ.
Christological title kept with ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ per confessional usage. Native reviewer to confirm vs. a filial ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ ਦਾ ਪੁੱਤਰ ('the Father's Son').
John 3:16 wording family; ਇਕਲੌਤਾ = 'one and only / only-born'.

glossary.gloss: the kingdom / reign of our Father (revered)
glossary.note: FHB renders 'kingdom of God' filially: ਰਾਜ 'kingdom, reign' of ਸਾਡੇ ਪਿਤਾ ਜੀ, not ਪਰਮੇਸ਼ੁਰ ਦਾ ਰਾਜ.
Standard Punjabi Bible spelling of the name.
The title as used in the Punjabi Bible; ਯਿਸੂ ਮਸੀਹ = 'Jesus Christ'.
ਮਸੀਹਾ marks the awaited-deliverer sense, distinguished from the compounded title ਮਸੀਹ; reviewer to confirm both read naturally.
Standard Punjabi Bible term for covenant (ਨੇਮ).
Renders Hebrew hesed (loyal covenant love). ਅਟੱਲ 'immovable, steadfast' + ਪ੍ਰੇਮ 'love'. Reviewer to weigh vs. ਪੱਕਾ ਪਿਆਰ or ਸਦਾ ਰਹਿਣ ਵਾਲਾ ਪ੍ਰੇਮ.
Renders racham / splanchnizomai (gut-level tender mercy). ਤਰਸ carries the felt, moved-with-pity sense; ਦਯਾ is the more formal alternative.
Punjabi Bible term; the abstract noun from ਧਰਮ (right/just conduct).
John 3:16 wording family; ਸਦੀਪਕ 'everlasting' + ਜੀਵਨ 'life' (vs. ਅਨੰਤ ਜੀਵਨ).

glossary.gloss: to be born anew / from the beginning
glossary.note: John 3:3,7 sense; ਨਵੇਂ ਸਿਰਿਓਂ 'afresh, from the start' + ਜੰਮਣਾ 'to be born' — holds the double meaning 'again / from above'.
